rationale_for_baseline_tier: A localised, non-systemic allergic contact dermatitis manageable with a prescription and allergen avoidance — routine/prompt GP handling with a secondary-infection safety-net; not urgent.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Spreading hot redness beyond the plaque, thick purulent discharge, or fever
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests secondary bacterial infection needing antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads and gets hot beyond the square, you see thick yellow pus, or you get a fever — get urgent medical review.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Provide clear written instructions and safety-net thresholds.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Stop wearing the metal belt against your skin and stop the antiseptic cream. Use the prescribed steroid ointment thinly once daily and an antihistamine for the itch. Watch for spreading redness, pus or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP for prescription/review; urgent care if infection develops
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP for the prescription; urgent care/ED for spreading infection or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document nickel/metal ACD with secondary excoriation, irritant antiseptic ceased, treatment started, and plan for allergen avoidance/patch testing.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Self-care only with no secondary-infection safety-net or allergen advice.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Excoriated skin superinfects and progresses to cellulitis before review.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No prescription or allergen-avoidance guidance, leaving the dermatitis to persist.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending to ED for a localised contact dermatitis without infection.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot patch-test or palpate remotely, but the contact-shaped plaque is diagnostic.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as nickel ACD with allergen avoidance, stop the irritant, prescribe a topical steroid, and safety-net for infection.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Mislabelling as tinea/impetigo or continuing the irritant antiseptic.
